label: Testing – Edge Cases
title: Testing Quiz Edge Cases
description: Synthetic fixture for regression testing all supported quiz question types and rendering combinations.

questions.label: Plain single-choice question with four options.
questions.explanation: Single-choice questions should accept exactly one radio answer.

questions.label: Single-choice question with boolean-like string values.
questions.explanation: Option values are strings even when they look like booleans.

questions.label: Single-choice question with numeric-like string values.
questions.explanation: The runtime should compare values as exact strings.

questions.label: Multiple-choice question with two correct answers.
questions.explanation: Multi-select scoring should require the exact selected set.

questions.label: Multiple-choice question with only one correct checkbox answer.
questions.explanation: A multi-select question may still have a single correct value.

questions.labelHtml: HTML label with <code>, symbols & escaped text.

options.text: Plain <not HTML> text should be escaped.

options.html: Correct option containing trusted HTML and inline code.

options.text: Diacritics: č, š, ž, ä, ô.

questions.explanation: The renderer supports labelHtml and option.html while escaping option.text.
Plain labels can contain fenced code blocks that render as code.

questions.label: Question with an image path that should be resolved relative to the set.
questions.imageAlt: Placeholder image for testing relative paths
questions.explanation: This checks image and imageAlt propagation. The placeholder path is intentionally synthetic.

questions.label: Question with custom image HTML.
questions.imageHtml: <svg role="img" aria-label="Inline test SVG" width="120" height="40"><rect width="120" height="40" fill="#f5f5f5" stroke="#222"/><text x="10" y="25">SVG</text></svg>

questions.label: Text answer with default contains-any matching.
questions.explanation: The runtime treats text questions without answerMode as contains_any.

questions.label: Text answer with explicit contains_any matching.
questions.explanation: This checks explicit contains_any plus HTML hint rendering.

questions.labelHtml: Text answer requiring all keywords in any order.
questions.hint: All three words must appear, but the order does not matter.
questions.explanation: contains_all should fail partial answers and pass when every keyword is present.

questions.label: Text answer requiring a sequence of ordered tokens.
questions.hint: The order matters for this one.
questions.explanation: sequence should catch answers with the right tokens in the wrong order.

questions.label: Text answer with punctuation, spaces, and diacritics.
questions.hint: Accepted keywords intentionally include punctuation and non-ASCII characters.
questions.explanation: Useful for checking keyword parsing and normalization.
